text.title: Abuse Case Test Cookbook Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: Does the abuse-case step lack a named exit criterion?
Is a decision branch missing an explicit signal?

conclusion.verdict: re-author with a named input signal (status code, error message, log line)

branch.question: Was a deviation made without one-line rationale?

conclusion.verdict: log it now; surface in next retro; do not normalize undocumented skips

branch.question: Does the playbook lack a named owner who can authorize amendments?

conclusion.verdict: assign one owner; orphaned playbooks rot

branch.question: Is the work outside reusable security-test territory (e.g. one-off pen-test)?

conclusion.verdict: use a single-engagement pen-test report instead
signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
